personal_info.name: Muhammad Hassan Siddiqui
personal_info.title: Principal Software Engineer
personal_info.tagline: Backend engineer specializing in Python, Django, and scalable web systems

about_me.text: {years}+ years of building backend systems that hold up in production — APIs, data pipelines, scrapers, and conversational AI services. Python and Django are home ground; I've shipped across ed-tech, sports analytics, and loyalty platforms. These days I lead teams, own architecture decisions, and mentor engineers through the same rabbit holes I came up through.

highlights: Lead architecture on client products including Toastmasters International's data migration platform and Supercal's AI scheduling service.
Design and ship complex backend features end-to-end — schema, APIs, task queues, and deployment.
Mentor junior engineers through their first production-scale Python and Django codebases.
Run technical planning and cross-team coordination through requirements, delivery, and release.
Designed and shipped APIs powering the Entertainer and Munch consumer apps.
Built Scrapy-based scrapers that pulled merchant and outlet data from multiple vendors.
Wrote scheduled jobs keeping merchant and outlet catalogs in sync with upstream sources.
Designed and implemented a loyalty system shared across multiple consumer products.
Designed backend architecture and APIs powering mobile apps and admin dashboards across multiple clients.
Engineered performance-scoring algorithms for basketball shooting drills in the Ballogy app.
Led the engineering team through planning, delivery, and cross-team coordination.
Owned production deployments and live-server monitoring for the team's products.
Shipped new features and change requests against client specifications.
Wrote unit and functional tests to keep the codebase stable across releases.
Contributed to NLP research projects at the Al-Khawarizmi Institute of Computer Science — work that led to a published paper on Punjabi Shahmukhi stemming.

projects.name: Toastmasters International — Data Migration
projects.description: Data migration platform for Toastmasters International — moving member, club, and program data from a legacy system to a new platform across a global network of clubs.
contributions: Built the migration pipelines that moved member, club, and program data off the legacy system.
Designed a rule-based validation engine that flagged data-quality issues and produced reports across data points.
Built a data-cleaning framework that enforced integrity before records reached the new platform.
Architected the migration to run at scale — batched, resumable, and safe to re-run on failures.

projects.name: Advance Learning World
projects.description: Ed-tech platform serving schools and individual learners with personalized learning paths, progress reporting, and recommendation tools.
contributions: Added multi-tenant support so schools could onboard as B2B clients alongside individual learners.
Shipped weekly progress reports for students and a separate reporting view for B2B teachers.
Built a recommendation engine that surfaced each student's weakest areas for focused practice.
Built a shared question bank spanning teacher and student workflows.
Built the end-of-year promotion system that advanced students to the next grade.

projects.name: Ballogy
projects.description: Sports analytics platform for basketball — skill assessment, development tracking, and coaching tools.
contributions: Built the shooting-test management system that captures and scores basketball drills.
Built the coach panel that lets coaches track, compare, and evaluate athletes.
Drove architecture decisions as the product grew in scope and traffic.
Scaled the backend to handle thousands of concurrent users and spiky test-day traffic.

projects.name: Supercal
projects.description: Email-based meeting scheduler that uses AI to coordinate group availability and eliminate the back-and-forth. Built with Django on AWS.
contributions: Designed and built the backend for the conversational AI module that parses email replies and drives scheduling decisions.
Built the conversational service that coordinates group availability over email threads.
Built the backend APIs supporting the rest of the product surface.

projects.name: Eaziex
projects.description: Classified-ads platform with a flexible, category-specific form and filter engine — each ad category defines its own fields and its own searchable filters.
contributions: Designed the full backend architecture from scratch.
Built a form-management system that lets admins compose a different ad form per category.
Built the matching filter engine so each category's ads could be searched by its own fields.
Built the customer-facing frontend in ReactJS and NextJS.

education.institution: Punjab University College of Information Technology
University of Engineering and Technology, Lahore

publications.authors: Mateen, Abdul, M. Kamran Malik, Zubair Nawaz, H.M. Danish, M. Hassan Siddiqui, and Qaiser Abbas
publications.title: A Hybrid Stemmer of Punjabi Shahmukhi Script
